data.title: HuFriedyGroup Announces Strategic Partnership with Seattle Study Club to Advance Dental Education and Clinical Excellence
data.description: Chicago, April 22, 2026 - HuFriedyGroup, a leading company in dental instrument manufacturing, infection prevention, and education solutions, has revealed a significant collaboration with Seattle Study Club, a renowned organization in dental education. This partnership brings together two prominent players in the industry to enhance their offerings. Through this alliance, HuFriedyGroup aims to further establish itself as a key player in the dental sector.

data.title: Zum Founder and CEO Ritu Narayan Named Entrepreneur Of The Year® 2026 Bay Area Finalist by EY US
data.description: Ritu Narayan, the Founder and CEO of Zūm, has been acknowledged for her exceptional leadership in developing a groundbreaking platform transforming student mobility for 26 million children throughout the United States. This recognition comes as Narayan is announced as a finalist for the Entrepreneur award.

data.title: TSMC Debuts A13 Technology at 2026 North America Technology Symposium
data.description: In Santa Clara, California, TSMC unveiled its latest advancement in cutting-edge process technology at the 2026 North America Technology Symposium. The new A13 process, a direct reduction of the previously acclaimed A14 node from 2025, offers more compact and efficient designs. This innovation caters to the soaring demand for computational power across various sectors like artificial intelligence, high-performance computing, and mobile devices. TSMC's stock is listed as TWSE: 2330 and NYSE: TSM.

data.title: Cadence Collaborates with TSMC to Accelerate Design of Next-Generation AI Silicon
data.description: A significant development has emerged in the tech sector as Cadence revealed an expansion of its partnership with TSMC. The collaboration, which involves Nasdaq-listed Cadence, seeks to propel AI-driven semiconductor advancements. This expanded initiative aims to provide crucial components such as IP, signoff-ready solutions, complete design infrastructure, and cutting-edge certified flows. These offerings are tailored for top-tier AI silicon and are compatible with TSMC's N3, N2, A16™, and A14 process technologies. By teaming up more extensively, the companies endeavor to aid customers in minimizing iterations and enhancing correlation concerning DTCO-focused advancements.
